title: rottboellia gabonensis
intro: Rottboellia gabonensis, also known as the African corn or African finger grass, is a type of tropical grass native to West and Central Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimalarial compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Rottboellia gabonensis has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions by boiling its roots or leaves in water, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
